title: oxalis cuneata
intro: Oxalis cuneata, also known as the pointed wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Oxalis cuneata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Oxalis cuneata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are typically made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
